title: net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer timeout hardcoded
description:
lichen_l2.c:606-608: net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer uses K_MSEC(10) hardcoded timeout with comment 'Use short timeout to allow memory reclaim under pressure.'

What goes wrong: 10ms is arbitrary. Under actual memory pressure, 10ms may be too short (allocation fails when a slightly longer wait would succeed) or too long (stalls RX processing for real-time requirements). There's no Kconfig option to tune this.

Contributors may copy this pattern without understanding the tradeoff.

Fix: Consider extracting to a CONFIG_ option or at least a named constant with documented rationale.
close_reason: Fixed: Extracted hardcoded K_MSEC(10) to CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_RX_ALLOC_TIMEOUT_MS Kconfig option with documented tradeoff rationale
